title: Theory of flight
subtitle: Short modules with a quiz at the end. Pass the quiz, then try it in the simulator.

quiz.title: Quiz
quiz.wrong: Not quite — the right answer is highlighted.
quiz.passed: Module passed — you are ready for the simulator!
quiz.failed: Review the lessons and try again.

principles-of-flight.title: Principles of flight
principles-of-flight.description: The four forces, the three axes of control, and why airplanes stay in the air.

four-forces.title: The four forces
four-forces.body: Four forces act on every aircraft: lift pushes it up, weight pulls it down, thrust moves it forward, and drag holds it back. Lift is created by the wings as air flows over them — the faster you fly, the more lift you generate. Straight and level flight is simply these four forces in balance.

three-axes.title: The three axes of control
three-axes.body: An aircraft rotates around three axes. Pitch (elevator) raises or lowers the nose, roll (ailerons) banks the wings, and yaw (rudder) points the nose left or right. To turn, pilots bank the wings and let the tilted lift pull the aircraft around the turn.

speed-and-stall.title: Throttle, airspeed and stall
speed-and-stall.body: The throttle controls engine power, and power controls your airspeed. Wings need a minimum airspeed to produce enough lift: fly slower than that and the wing stalls — it stops lifting and the nose drops. To recover from a stall, lower the nose, apply full throttle and let the speed build up again.

q1.question: Which force keeps an aircraft in the air?

q2.question: Banking the wings with the ailerons is called…

q3.question: What happens if airspeed drops below stall speed?
options: The wing stops producing enough lift

q4.question: How do you recover from a stall?
options: Lower the nose and apply full throttle
Pull the nose up as hard as you can
Cut the throttle and hold the stick still

cockpit-instruments.title: Cockpit instruments
cockpit-instruments.description: Altimeter, airspeed indicator and attitude indicator — reading the panel at a glance.
